methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Isomorphic Labs is a private-stage biotech company founded by Demis Hassabis that has raised $600M to date. The comparable universe in Biotech & Health spans 3 public equities (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics, though none form a sufficiently tight peer cohort for reliable valuation anchoring.

Peer-Median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables; 16 same-sector peers exist but none satisfy narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier). Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because Isomorphic's revenue is not on file—standard practice for private companies. The public Biotech & Health sector median trades at 5.8x P/S across the three tickers identified, but without Isomorphic's revenue this multiple cannot be meaningfully deployed.

Given the absence of applicable quantitative triangulation methods, this valuation cannot be supported by the data on hand. A credible range would require either: (1) disclosure of Isomorphic's annual revenue or revenue run-rate; (2) identification of 3+ truly comparable private rounds at similar stage and capitalization; or (3) forward-revenue projections tied to disclosed pipeline milestones. The $600M raised establishes a floor for historical cost basis but does not establish enterprise value. Further diligence or disclosure is necessary before a defensible valuation can be anchored.
